description: trust set — full option test coverage for --account/--keystore/--password, --clear-no-ripple, --quality-in/--quality-out, --freeze/--unfreeze, and --auth

userStories.title: Tests for --account/--keystore/--password, --clear-no-ripple, --quality-in, --quality-out
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the simpler missing options covered so keystore-based signing and quality fields are verified on-chain.
acceptanceCriteria: Add test '--account + --keystore + --password': use 'wallet new --save --password pw123 --keystore <tmpdir> --json' to create a keystore entry, then run 'trust set --currency USD --issuer <issuer-address> --limit 1000 --account <address> --keystore <tmpdir> --password pw123 --node testnet', assert exit 0 and stdout contains 'tesSUCCESS'; clean up tmpdir in finally block

Add test '--clear-no-ripple on-chain effect': first run trust set with --currency CNY --no-ripple on trustor, then run trust set with --currency CNY --clear-no-ripple on the same trust line; query 'account trust-lines --json <trustor-address>', find CNY line and assert no_ripple is false or absent (!line.no_ripple)

Add test '--quality-in and --quality-out': run trust set --currency JPY --issuer <issuer> --limit 10000 --quality-in 950000000 --quality-out 950000000 --seed <trustor-seed> --node testnet; query 'account trust-lines --json <trustor-address>', find JPY line and assert quality_in === 950000000 and quality_out === 950000000
All new tests added to tests/e2e/trust/trust.test.ts alongside existing tests
Use existing trustor and issuer wallets already funded in beforeAll — do not fund new accounts
Use currency codes CNY, JPY to avoid conflicts with existing tests (USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, MXN, XYZ already used)

userStories.title: Tests for --freeze, --unfreeze, and --auth
userStories.description: As a developer, I want issuer-side flag operations tested so freeze/unfreeze and trust line authorization are verified on-chain.
acceptanceCriteria: Add test '--freeze': trustor runs trust set --currency FRZ --issuer <issuer-address> --limit 1000 --seed <trustor-seed> to establish the line; issuer then runs trust set --currency FRZ --issuer <trustor-address> --limit 0 --freeze --seed <issuer-seed>; query 'account trust-lines --json <trustor-address>', find FRZ line and assert freeze_peer === true

Add test '--unfreeze': issuer runs trust set --currency FRZ --issuer <trustor-address> --limit 0 --unfreeze --seed <issuer-seed>; query trust lines and assert the FRZ line no longer has freeze_peer: true

Add test '--auth': in test body (using xrpl.js Client directly), issuer submits AccountSet tx with SetFlag: AccountSetAsfFlags.asfRequireAuth (value 2) to enable RequireAuth; trustor creates trust set --currency AUT --issuer <issuer-address> --limit 100 --seed <trustor-seed>; issuer authorizes with trust set --currency AUT --issuer <trustor-address> --limit 0 --auth --seed <issuer-seed>; query 'account trust-lines --json <trustor-address>', find AUT line and assert peer_authorized === true
All new tests added to tests/e2e/trust/trust.test.ts
Use existing trustor and issuer wallets from beforeAll
Use currency codes FRZ and AUT (not already used by other tests)
Import AccountSetAsfFlags from xrpl package for the --auth test setup
